description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a robust metal frame. The panels are elevated above a base structure that appears to house electrical components, possibly including an inverter and wiring. The mounting frame consists of angled metal supports, providing stability and optimal tilt for sunlight capture. The base is boxy and solid, likely constructed from metal and possibly concrete, with visible surface details and some red and black coloration. The solar panels themselves have a dark, glassy surface typical of photovoltaic cells, bordered by metallic frames. The overall design suggests a ground-mounted solar array suitable for urban environments such as parking lots, sidewalks, or small parks.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array with a sturdy metal frame and a solid base, featuring dark photovoltaic panels and visible support structures.
description_view_0: Front view showing the face of the solar panels and the solid base structure beneath.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the angled metal supports and the elongated base, with the panel array elevated above.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the base and the underside of the solar panels, with support structures visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the base and the supporting frame beneath the solar panels.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20-30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with angled steel or aluminum frame supports
attribute_array_configuration: single linear row of approximately 4 panels
